terms.definition: A highly contagious viral infection of dogs causing haemorrhagic gastroenteritis.
terms.exampleSentence: Parvovirus was confirmed by faecal antigen testing in the unvaccinated puppy.
terms.contextNotes: Vaccination from 6–8 weeks is the cornerstone of prevention.

terms.definition: A serious viral disease of dogs causing respiratory, gastrointestinal and neurological signs.
terms.exampleSentence: Distemper should be considered in any unvaccinated dog with myoclonus.
terms.contextNotes: Hyperkeratosis ('hard pad') is a classical sign.

terms.definition: An infectious tracheobronchitis of dogs, commonly involving Bordetella bronchiseptica and viruses.
terms.exampleSentence: Kennel cough spread rapidly through the boarding facility last week.
terms.contextNotes: Intranasal vaccines are commonly used pre-boarding.

terms.definition: Feline immunodeficiency virus, a lentivirus causing progressive immunosuppression in cats.
terms.exampleSentence: The cat's FIV-positive status warranted strict indoor housing.
terms.contextNotes: Transmitted predominantly by deep bite wounds.

terms.definition: Feline leukaemia virus, a retrovirus causing immunosuppression and neoplasia in cats.
terms.exampleSentence: FeLV infection was identified on a combined antigen test in the new kitten.
terms.contextNotes: Spread mainly by friendly contact (grooming, sharing bowls).

terms.definition: Excessive thyroid hormone production, usually from a benign adenoma, in older cats.
terms.exampleSentence: Hyperthyroidism in cats classically presents with weight loss despite polyphagia.
terms.contextNotes: Treatment options include medical, surgical and I-131.

terms.definition: Cushing's syndrome; excess cortisol from pituitary or adrenal disease.
terms.exampleSentence: Hyperadrenocorticism was suspected following polyuria and a pendulous abdomen.
terms.contextNotes: Trilostane is the most common medical therapy.

terms.definition: Hypoadrenocorticism; deficient adrenocortical hormone production.
terms.exampleSentence: Addison's disease was confirmed by a flat ACTH stimulation response.
terms.contextNotes: Classic 'great pretender'; consider with hyperkalaemia and hyponatraemia.

terms.definition: Gastric dilatation-volvulus; acute gastric distension with rotation, common in deep-chested dogs.
terms.exampleSentence: GDV was confirmed radiographically and the dog was taken to surgery immediately.
terms.contextNotes: Time-critical surgical emergency.
commonMistakes: confusing with simple bloat in cattle

terms.definition: Intervertebral disc disease; herniation of the intervertebral disc causing spinal cord compression.
terms.exampleSentence: IVDD was suspected after the dachshund developed acute paraparesis.
terms.contextNotes: Chondrodystrophic breeds such as dachshunds are predisposed.

terms.definition: Developmental malformation of the coxofemoral joint leading to laxity and osteoarthritis.
terms.exampleSentence: Hip dysplasia was graded moderate on the BVA/KC scoring scheme.
terms.contextNotes: BVA/KC scheme is the UK reference.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the mammary gland, usually due to bacterial infection, in dairy cows.
terms.exampleSentence: Mastitis in dairy cattle was managed with intramammary antibiotics and culture-guided therapy.
terms.contextNotes: Major cause of economic loss in dairy production.

terms.definition: Painful inflammation of the laminae of the hoof, most commonly in horses and ponies.
terms.exampleSentence: Laminitis was suspected when the pony adopted a classic 'sawhorse' stance.
terms.contextNotes: Often linked with equine metabolic syndrome and PPID.

terms.definition: Abdominal pain in horses, ranging from mild spasmodic episodes to surgical lesions.
terms.exampleSentence: Colic in horses requires prompt assessment to identify surgical cases early.
terms.contextNotes: Heart rate, mucous membranes and rectal findings guide triage.

terms.definition: Feline lower urinary tract disease; a syndrome of dysuria, haematuria and pollakiuria in cats.
terms.exampleSentence: FLUTD was diagnosed after the male cat presented with stranguria.
terms.contextNotes: Obstructive FLUTD in male cats is an emergency.

terms.definition: Generalised dilatation and reduced motility of the oesophagus.
terms.exampleSentence: Megaesophagus was confirmed on contrast radiography after chronic regurgitation.
terms.contextNotes: Aspiration pneumonia is a major complication.

terms.definition: An accumulation of purulent material within the uterus, typically of entire bitches and queens.
terms.exampleSentence: Pyometra was diagnosed on ultrasound and an emergency ovariohysterectomy was performed.
terms.contextNotes: Closed pyometra carries higher risk of sepsis.

terms.definition: Osteochondritis dissecans; a developmental joint disease causing cartilage flap formation.
terms.exampleSentence: OCD lesions of the shoulder were identified arthroscopically.
terms.contextNotes: Common in large-breed dogs aged 4–10 months.
commonMistakes: confusing with the human psychiatric OCD

terms.definition: A hereditary predisposition to allergic disease, commonly causing pruritus in dogs.
terms.exampleSentence: Atopy was suspected after seasonal pruritus failed to respond to flea control.
terms.contextNotes: Lifelong management; immunotherapy is disease-modifying.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the external ear canal.
terms.exampleSentence: Otitis externa was treated after cytology revealed Malassezia overgrowth.
terms.contextNotes: Always identify and address underlying causes (e.g. atopy).

terms.definition: A zoonotic bacterial infection caused by Leptospira spp., affecting kidneys and liver.
terms.exampleSentence: Leptospirosis was suspected following acute kidney injury in an unvaccinated dog.
terms.contextNotes: Important zoonosis; counsel owners on PPE.

terms.definition: A tick-borne protozoal disease of dogs caused by Babesia spp., leading to haemolytic anaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Canine babesiosis was suspected after travel to an endemic area and pale mucous membranes.
terms.contextNotes: Take a careful travel and tick-exposure history.

terms.definition: A poorly understood dysautonomia of grazing horses, often fatal.
terms.exampleSentence: Equine grass sickness was suspected after rapid weight loss and gastric reflux.
terms.contextNotes: Most common in Northern Europe, especially the UK.

terms.definition: A contagious bacterial infection of the interdigital skin and hoof of sheep.
terms.exampleSentence: Foot rot in sheep was managed with footbathing and isolation of lame ewes.
terms.contextNotes: Caused mainly by Dichelobacter nodosus with Fusobacterium necrophorum.

terms.definition: A highly contagious parvoviral disease of cats causing severe leukopenia and enteritis.
terms.exampleSentence: Feline panleukopenia was confirmed in the unvaccinated kitten with profound leukopenia.
terms.contextNotes: Core vaccination prevents most cases.

terms.definition: Feline infectious peritonitis; a fatal coronavirus-associated immune-mediated disease of cats.
terms.exampleSentence: FIP was suspected in the young cat with abdominal effusion and uveitis.
terms.contextNotes: Antiviral therapy with GS-441524 has transformed outcomes.

terms.definition: Periparturient hypocalcaemia in dairy cows around calving.
terms.exampleSentence: Milk fever was suspected in the recumbent freshly calved cow with cold extremities.
terms.contextNotes: Treated with intravenous calcium borogluconate.

terms.definition: A zoonotic dermatophyte infection of the skin, hair and nails.
terms.exampleSentence: Ringworm was confirmed by fungal culture in the kitten with circular alopecia.
terms.contextNotes: Important zoonosis; counsel on hygiene.

terms.definition: A severe poxviral disease of rabbits transmitted by biting insects and direct contact.
terms.exampleSentence: Myxomatosis was diagnosed in the unvaccinated rabbit with periocular swelling.
terms.contextNotes: Combined myxo/RHD vaccination is recommended in the UK.

terms.definition: A chronic endocrine disease of dogs characterised by insulin deficiency and hyperglycaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Diabetes mellitus in dogs was diagnosed after persistent fasting hyperglycaemia and glucosuria.
terms.contextNotes: Most diabetic dogs require lifelong twice-daily insulin.
